name: Views of the Bardenas Reales — return via the Ebro River - from Alfaro
name_original: Vistas de las Bardenas Reales — vuelta por el Río Ebro - desde Alfaro
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alfaro offer diverse terrain, from the flat plains of the Ebro and Alhama River Valleys to undulating hills and vineyards. The region provides access to more challenging mountainous areas, including Mount Yerga, for cyclists seeking ascents. Quiet country lanes traverse the landscape, ensuring a tranquil riding experience through varied topography. Best road cycling routes around Alfaro The most popular road cycling route is Views of the Bardenas Reales — return via the Ebro River - from Alfaro, a 66.8 miles (107.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views and follows the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Francisco Church – St. Michael's Collegiate Church loop from Alfaro, a moderate 40.3 miles (64.9 km) path. This loop connects historic churches within the Alfaro area. Local road cyclists also love the Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop from Alfaro, a 68.9 miles (110.9 km) trail leading through the Alhama River valley, often completed in about 5 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Alfaro is defined by river valleys, extensive vineyards, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding ascents. The routes in Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alfaro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Alfaro?
A: Alfaro offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find flat plains along the Ebro and Alhama River Valleys, undulating hills, and extensive vineyards. For those seeking more challenging ascents, the region also provides access to mountainous areas like Mount Yerga. The majority of roads are quiet country lanes, ensuring a tranquil riding experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alfaro?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Alfaro listed on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 5 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, offering plenty of options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available in Alfaro. The flat sections along the Ebro and Alhama riverbanks also offer relaxed cycling experiences, ideal for beginners or those looking for a leisurely ride through scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Alfaro?
A: Absolutely. Alfaro and the surrounding La Rioja region feature more demanding terrain, including ascents up Mount Yerga. There are 6 difficult routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: What scenic views or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: Many routes offer picturesque views of the Ebro and Alhama River Valleys, vast vineyards, and the historic town of Alfaro itself. You can pass by cultural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt;, known for its stork colony, or natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotos del Ebro Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also provide panoramic vistas from higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Alfaro?
A: Yes, several routes around Alfaro are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31476515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Church – St. Michael's Collegiate Church loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 40.3-mile (64.9 km) path connecting historic churches, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, more difficult option.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alfaro?
A: The road cycling routes in Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from tranquil river valleys to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to ride through the beautiful vineyards on quiet country lanes.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, Alfaro offers several long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990898985/views-of-the-bardenas-reales-return-via-the-ebro-river-from-alfaro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Bardenas Reales — return via the Ebro River - from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 66.8-mile (107.4 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; covers 68.9 miles (110.9 km), providing excellent options for extended rides.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or places to stop along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with villages every few kilometers, offering opportunities for refreshment. Given Alfaro's location in the La Rioja wine region, you can often find wineries and local gastronomy, including tapas and pintxos, to enjoy during your ride.

Q: Is Alfaro suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designated road cycling routes are not explicitly detailed, the flatter sections along the Ebro and Alhama River Valleys provide relaxed and scenic cycling experiences that could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances on paved roads. Always check the route's difficulty and distance before heading out.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Alfaro?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for cycling. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for exploring the vineyards and river valleys. Summers can be warm, especially in the plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church – Cornago Castle loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that leads towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt; and the historic Cornago Castle, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.
tips: Funes is a destination with great diversity in a compact area. It offers history (Roman and medieval), stunning natural beauty (ravines, rivers, viewpoints), a unique winemaking heritage, and excellent hiking and biking trails without the need for long journeys. The contrast between the robust heritage and the serenity of the riverside landscape creates an immersive and authentic experience. Ideal for those seeking a blend of cultural and natural activities, whether as a couple, in a group, or alone.

It is located at Plaza de la Cava, 1, in the town center of Marcilla (Navarra), easily accessible and surrounded by bars and a children's playground.
This 15th-century Gothic fortress was built of brick on a stone foundation by Mosén Pierres de Peralta, with material and financial support from Charles III the Noble.
It served both as a defensive stronghold and as a palatial residence for the Marquesses of Falces, descendants of Peralta.
In 1516, the Marchioness Ana de Velasco saved the castle from being demolished by order of Cardinal Cisneros.

Arguedas (Navarra), a charming place that transcends being a gateway to the Bardenas Reales:
The town center is welcoming, ideal for strolling through its streets and discovering historic buildings such as the Town Hall and the Casa Muruzábal Palace (16th-18th centuries).
Two chapels stand out: the Virgen del Yugo (Gothic-Renaissance style, 17th century, on a hill with panoramic views) and the Hermitage of San Miguel (16th century).

The Ebro Bridge in Tudela is an impressive example of medieval architecture. Built of stone, it spans 360 meters and consists of 16 piers and 17 uneven arches. This bridge is fundamental to the history of Tudela, as it has been a strategic crossing over the Ebro River since its construction in the 12th and 13th centuries.

Although tradition attributes its construction to King Sancho VII the Strong, some historians suggest it may be a reconstruction of an earlier Muslim bridge. Over the years, it has undergone numerous renovations to withstand floods and the ravages of time.

Corella is a highly recommended destination for:

Lovers of Baroque art who want to enjoy perfectly preserved churches, palaces, and convents.
Nature enthusiasts who enjoy walks along the river, lagoons, and riverside trails.
Those who appreciate the local, authentic culture, alive in festivals and traditions.
Food tourists seeking traditional dishes made with high-quality ingredients.
